summary: Validated termografia import fix using real /tmp/todas.xlsx. Backend now imports 200 termo records (each TAG row, subconjunto optional) and vibracao remains at 215 composite-tag machines. Reimport is idempotent (inserted=0, skipped=200). Frontend gating fixed: admin sees Importar Lista + 200 cards (incl. QDE-229.01, TRF-0001); viewer correctly hides import-termo-btn (count=0) while still seeing the 200 read-only cards.
passed_backend: GET /api/machines?tipo=termografia returns 200 records; sample TAGs QDE-229.01 and TRF-0001 present; tipo=termografia; tag = raw TAG; _id excluded
Reimport of /tmp/todas.xlsx is idempotent: inserted=0, skipped=200
GET /api/machines?tipo=vibracao still returns 215 composite-tag machines ({parent_tag} / {subconjunto}) — vibracao path unbroken
Prior regression suite /app/backend/tests/test_vibracao_termo.py — 8/8 still passing (template, ISO alarm classification, RBAC delete viewer/admin, point/clear, synthetic termo sheet)
passed_frontend: Admin /termografia: 200 termo-card-* rendered; termo-card-TRF-0001 and termo-card-QDE-229.01 both present (count=1 each)
Viewer /termografia: import-termo-btn HIDDEN (count=0) — gating fix from iteration_5 confirmed
Viewer /termografia: still sees 200 termo cards (read-only flow OK)
Sidebar correctly hides admin items (Plantas, Biblioteca Defeitos, Usuários) for viewer
critical_code_review_comments: import_machines() in server.py is ~100 lines with branching by tipo — readable now but consider extracting termo vs vibracao row handlers when adding 'ambos' support so the SRP stays clean.
Stub-machine auto-creation on /api/measurements/import (carry-over from iter5 review) still applies — masks miscadastro; consider a strict_match flag.
Termografia.js upload error handler ignores HTTP status. A 413/500 response will surface the same generic 'Falha ao importar' toast. Surface err.response?.data?.detail for better UX.
test_credentials: Admin admin@fssolucoes.com/admin123, Viewer viewer@fs.com/viewer123 — both functional
seed_data_creation: /tmp/todas.xlsx was imported into DB (200 termografia + 215 vibracao machines). These are the user's intended production-like dataset and were NOT cleaned up — reimport is idempotent so this is safe.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression for termo/vibracao import lives in /app/backend/tests/test_termo_real_import.py (uses /tmp/todas.xlsx) and /app/backend/tests/test_vibracao_termo.py (synthetic). Run: `pytest backend/tests/ -v -o addopts=` from /app. DB is already seeded with 200 termo + 215 vibracao after this iteration.
